for r in range(14, 25):

</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I can see the player character (black with white dots) has moved further right. It's now straddling the divider. The boundary is `[[15, 24], [15, 32], ...]` which means it spans from col 24 to col 32, crossing the divider (cols 30-32).
The dark gray T-shape (id=6) has also moved - it's now at cols 33-38 (was at 33-41 before, so it moved right by some amount). Wait, let me reconsider.
Actually looking at the ASCII more carefully:
- Row 15: `bbbbBBBBBBBBBccccccb` - the black shape is at cols 24-32, and the dark gray shape is at cols 33-39
- Row 16: `bbbbBWBBWBBSBccccccb` - the player with white dots is at cols 24-32
So the player moved right again (from cols 21-29 to cols 24-32), and the T-shape also moved right (from cols 33-41 to cols 33-38... wait that doesn't make sense).
Let me reconsider. The T-shape (dark gray, id=6) now has only 18 pixels (was 45). Looking at the boundary `[[15, 33], [15, 38], [17, 38], [17, 33]]`, it's a 3x5 rectangle at the top part only. The bottom stem of the T seems to have been cut off or moved.
Wait - looking at the ASCII row 16: `bbbbBWBBWBBSBccccccb`
- The 'B' at position 31 (within the substring starting at col 20) corresponds to col 31
- The 'S' at positions 30-32 is the divider
Actually, I think what's happening is the player character is moving through the divider and merging/interacting with the T-shape. The T-shape is being consumed or modified as the player moves through it.
Let me try more RIGHT actions to see if the player can fully cross to the right side.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The player is now crossing the divider. Let me try more RIGHT actions
# to see if it can fully reach the right side
action(['RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
